events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Scenario: Cable tray fill ratio exceeded, rough-complete, NEC Art 392. Need decide fail because non-compliant. Need fill ratio value? Not given numeric reading. Could set value to 0 or maybe fill ratio exceeded unknown. Need component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Risk: high? Overfill can cause overheating, installation damage, code violation. Industrial site. Maybe medium/high. Since fill ratio exceeded violates NEC 392 and could cause cable overheating/damage, risk high. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80. Findings: cable tray fill ratio exceeds NEC maximum allowable. Actions: remove/rearrange cables to within allowable fill, verify calculations, reinspect. Rationale. References: NEC Article 392. Need include all fields. Sound etc default 0/empty. Workable empty. Let's craft JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["Cable tray fill ratio exceeds the maximum permitted by NEC Article 392.", "Overfilled tray can cause overheating, cable damage, and difficulty maintaining/replacing cables."],
  "actions": ["Remove or reroute excess cables to bring tray fill within NEC Article 392 limits.", "Verify fill calculations against tray width, cable diameters, and allowable area.", "Reinspect and document compliance before closing out the work."],
  "rationale": "NEC Article 392 limits cable tray fill based on cable type, tray type, and width. Exceeding the allowable fill ratio is a code violation and creates overheating and maintenance hazards, so the installation fails compliance at the rough-complete stage.",
  "references": ["NEC Article 392"]
}
